MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Tuner & Analysis Tools

Source: MOTU product page

The Analysis tab in CueMix FX provides a suite of real-time meters and diagnostic tools. All run in hardware via the MOTU's internal DSP, adding no computer CPU load.

FFT Display

Real-time full-screen frequency spectrum analyser. The horizontal axis is frequency (20 Hz–20 kHz); the vertical axis is amplitude. Use it to:

  • Identify resonant frequencies in the Hydrasynth stereo output that may need EQ reduction before mixing.
  • Check if the SM58 pickup has problematic room modes (narrow spikes in the low-mid range) that need a targeted parametric notch.
  • Verify that the hi-pass filter in CueMix FX EQ is actually removing sub-bass energy from the vocal channel.

Spectrogram Waterfall

A time-plus-frequency display that scrolls over time, showing where energy appears in the frequency domain moment-to-moment. Excellent for checking reverb tails — if the hall reverb extends too long in the low-mid range, it shows clearly as coloured energy persisting after the source note ends.

Oscilloscope

Shows the actual waveform shape of the signal in real time. Use it to:

  • Verify the VT-3 output doesn't have DC offset (a waveform centred above or below zero).
  • Check that the SM58 signal shape is clean and not clipping (visible as flat tops on waveform peaks).

X-Y Plot (Lissajous Figure)

Plots the left channel on the X axis and right channel on the Y axis. The shape reveals stereo content:

  • Diagonal line (45°): both channels are identical — perfectly mono signal.
  • Diagonal ellipse: normal stereo with some correlation — will collapse well to mono.
  • Wide horizontal blob: very wide stereo field; may lose information when collapsed to mono. Verify mono compatibility before printing.
  • X-shaped or horizontal: suggests phase issues — investigate cabling or processing chain.

Check the HS7 output's X-Y figure to verify stereo correlation of the final mix before bouncing.

Chromatic Tuner

A chromatic tuner that monitors any selected input in real time. Before each session:

  • Route the Hydrasynth mono output to a MOTU input.
  • Play a reference note (A3) on the Hydrasynth.
  • Tune the Hydrasynth's Master Tuning to A=440 Hz using the tuner display.
  • If using the P-225 alongside the Hydrasynth, ensure both instruments are tuned to the same reference before the session starts.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
